mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals tightening labor market and rising wage pressures, which compress corporate profit margins and reduce equity valuations. This negative earnings outlook produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current UNRATE at 6.6% suggests labor market slack
failure_condition: Rank IC > 0.05 over rolling 2-year windows, or SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE declines by > 0.2pp, contradicting the margin-compression mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly serves as a leading indicator of aggregate demand strength. Accelerating real GDP growth signals robust corporate revenue expansion and supports equity valuations over a 60-day forward horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with quarterly GDPC1 growth rates (Spearman rho < 0.15, p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above 4.5% signals restrictive monetary policy that raises the cost of capital for equity valuations and dampens economic activity expectations. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC during high-rate regimes remains below 0.06 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth reflects real economic capacity utilization and supply-side strength. Rising INDPRO signals improving corporate operating leverage and supports equity returns over a 5-day horizon as market reprices growth expectations upward.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.08 over rolling 2-year windows, or 5-day forward returns show no significant positive association with monthly INDPRO changes (Spearman rho < 0.12, p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal consumer demand strength and housing market health. When both series accelerate simultaneously, forward equity returns are positive over 20 days due to improved earnings visibility and reduced recession risk.
failure_condition: Rank IC for the interaction term < 0.07 over any 2-year rolling window, or joint acceleration of RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 20d returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth combined with rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals wage-price spiral dynamics. When both accelerate, equity returns turn negative over 60 days as markets price in future monetary tightening and margin compression.
regime_hint: inflationary expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: Rank IC for the interaction term < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of instances where both PAYEMS and CPIAUCSL accelerate.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) rising above 3.5% year-over-year triggers expectations of sustained Federal Reserve tightening. This regime shift produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days as equity risk premiums expand.
failure_condition: When CPI YoY > 3.5%, SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC during high-inflation regimes remains below 0.05 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) serve as a leading indicator of residential investment and construction employment. Sustained HOUST above 1.0M units signals robust housing demand and forward economic momentum, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST > 1.0M (3-month avg), SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 45% of triggered instances, or Rank IC during high-housing regimes remains below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.
